page: Microsoft Azure E8bds_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance belonging to the EBDSv5 family. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a base frequency of 2.8 GHz. The instance features 8 vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores with Hyper-Threading enabled, providing dedicated CPU allocation. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. The hardware configuration also includes 307 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage.

Benchmark evaluations place the E8bds_v5 in the average performance tier across most compute and memory operations. The instance achieved a Geekbench single-core score of 1622.0 and a multi-core score of 6090.0. Memory subsystem performance is characterized by a read-only small block cached bandwidth of 417.1 GB/sec and a large block uncached write bandwidth of 40.64 GB/sec. In database-centric workloads, the server processed 7.99 million Redis SET operations per second, reflecting solid memory throughput.

While the instance demonstrates stable performance for standard operations, certain multi-threaded and complex computational tasks reveal performance limitations. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression scored in the lower 25th percentile with a throughput of 31.23 GB/sec. Similarly, while the server handles small 135M parameter language models adequately, attempting LLM inference on medium 7B parameter models results in weak performance, yielding only 17.54 tokens/sec for prompt processing and 7.83 tokens/sec for text generation. The instance lacks GPU acceleration, making it unsuitable for heavy deep learning or large-scale AI inference.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E8bds_v5 provides a balanced allocation of high memory density and local storage without requiring the purchase of excess compute cores. This resource profile is highly suitable for memory-intensive applications, relational databases, caching layers like Redis, and moderate web serving workloads that benefit from local scratch disk space and premium storage capabilities.
description: Microsoft Azure E8bds_v5 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine instance from the EBDSv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 8 dedicated vCPUs, a 2.8 GHz clock speed, and 64.0 GB of RAM, establishing an 8.0 GB per vCPU ratio. The instance includes 307 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage. Performance benchmarks generally fall within the average range, highlighted by a Geekbench single-core score of 1622.0 and 7.99 million Redis SET operations per second. However, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and medium-sized LLM inference show weaker performance. Lacking a GPU, this instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-bound workloads rather than heavy parallel computing. It is best suited for database hosting, caching layers, and memory-intensive enterprise applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E8bds_v5 is an x86_64 memory-optimized instance featuring 8 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 307 GB local storage, designed for database workloads and memory-intensive applications.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E8bds_v5 is an 8-vCPU memory-optimized instance with 64 GB RAM and 307 GB local storage, powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C CPU.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, high memory-to-core density, and integrated local storage for database workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 8 dedicated vCPUs and 64 GB RAM
High memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core
Includes 307 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Delivers 7.99 million Redis SET operations per second in database benchmarks
Exhibits performance limitations in multi-threaded decompression and LLM inference
Optimized for relational databases, caching layers, and memory-intensive workloads
